---
title: "TSA final project"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-04-12"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(forecast)  
library(tseries)
library(tidyverse)
library(smooth)
library(dplyr)

```

# Introduction, Motivation, Relevance, Objectives

# Data Set Description

```{r}
#Importing time series data from text file
load_temp_data <- read.csv(
  file="./caiso_load_temp_final.csv",
  header=TRUE)

head(load_temp_data)

load_temp_data$date <- as.Date(load_temp_data$date)
load_temp_data <- load_temp_data %>% arrange(date)
```


# Methods


# Result
## Model 1 (Baseline): SARIMA

```{r}
# Split dataset
train_data <- load_temp_data %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2021-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2024-12-31"))

valid_data <- load_temp_data %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2025-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2025-12-31"))

train_ts <- ts(train_data$daily_peak_load, frequency = 7)

valid_ts <- ts(
  valid_data$daily_peak_load,
  start = end(train_ts) + c(0, 1),
  frequency = frequency(train_ts)
)
```

```{r}
fit_sarima <- auto.arima(train_ts, seasonal = TRUE)
summary(fit_sarima)

# validation 
forecast_2025 <- forecast(fit_sarima, h = length(valid_ts))
accuracy(forecast_2025, as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))

autoplot(forecast_2025) +
  autolayer(ts(valid_data$daily_peak_load,
               start = end(train_ts) + c(0,1),
               frequency = frequency(train_ts)),
            series = "Actual",
            color = "red") +
  xlab("Date") +
  ylab("Daily Peak Load") +
  ggtitle("SARIMA (Baseline): Forecast vs Actual (2025)")

```

```{r}
plot_df_sarima <- valid_data %>%
  mutate(forecast = as.numeric(forecast_2025$mean))

ggplot(plot_df_sarima, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = daily_peak_load, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = forecast, color = "Forecast"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = "Baseline SARIMA: Forecast vs Actual (2025)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Daily Peak Load",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Model 2: SARIMAX

```{r}
xreg_train <- as.matrix(train_data$tmax_mean)
xreg_valid <- as.matrix(valid_data$tmax_mean)

sum(is.na(xreg_train))
sum(is.na(xreg_valid))

# Fit model
fit_sarimax <- auto.arima(train_ts,
                          xreg = xreg_train,
                          seasonal = TRUE)
summary(fit_sarimax)

forecast_sarimax_2025 <- forecast(fit_sarimax,
                            xreg = xreg_valid,
                            h = nrow(valid_data))
accuracy(forecast_sarimax_2025, as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))

autoplot(forecast_sarimax_2025) +
  autolayer(ts(valid_data$daily_peak_load,
               start = end(train_ts) + c(0,1),
               frequency = frequency(train_ts)),
            series = "Actual",
            color = "red") +
  xlab("Date") +
  ylab("Daily Peak Load") +
  ggtitle("SARIMAX: Forecast vs Actual (2025)")



```

```{r}
plot_df_sarimax <- valid_data %>%
  mutate(forecast = as.numeric(forecast_sarimax_2025$mean))

ggplot(plot_df_sarimax, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = daily_peak_load, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = forecast, color = "Forecast"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = "SARIMAX: Forecast vs Actual (2025)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Daily Peak Load",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Model 3: Sarimax + Extreme heat dummy

```{r}
# Set the percentile of extreme heat
heat_threshold <- quantile(load_temp_data$tmax_mean, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)

load_temp_data$extreme_heat <- ifelse(
  load_temp_data$tmax_mean >= heat_threshold, 1, 0
)

table(load_temp_data$extreme_heat)
heat_threshold

train_data <- load_temp_data %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2021-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2024-12-31"))

valid_data <- load_temp_data %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2025-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2025-12-31"))

train_ts <- ts(train_data$daily_peak_load, frequency = 7)

xreg_train_m3 <- cbind(
  tmax = train_data$tmax_mean,
  extreme = train_data$extreme_heat
)

xreg_valid_m3 <- cbind(
  tmax = valid_data$tmax_mean,
  extreme = valid_data$extreme_heat
)

fit_sarimax_m3 <- auto.arima(
  train_ts,
  xreg = xreg_train_m3,
  seasonal = TRUE
)

summary(fit_sarimax_m3)

forecast_sarimax_m3_2025 <- forecast(
  fit_sarimax_m3,
  xreg = xreg_valid_m3,
  h = nrow(valid_data)
)

accuracy(forecast_sarimax_m3_2025, as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))

autoplot(forecast_sarimax_m3_2025) +
  autolayer(ts(valid_data$daily_peak_load,
               start = end(train_ts) + c(0,1),
               frequency = frequency(train_ts)),
            series = "Actual",
            color = "red") +
  xlab("Date") +
  ylab("Daily Peak Load") +
  ggtitle("SARIMAX + extreme heat dummy: Forecast vs Actual (2025)")
```

```{r}
plot_df_sarimax_dummy <- valid_data %>%
  mutate(forecast = as.numeric(forecast_sarimax_m3_2025$mean))

ggplot(plot_df_sarimax_dummy, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = daily_peak_load, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = forecast, color = "Forecast"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = "SARIMAX + Extreme Heat Dummy: Forecast vs Actual (2025)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Daily Peak Load",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Model 4: TBATS

```{r}
# Create multi-seasonal ts with weekly + annual cycles
train_msts <- msts(train_data$daily_peak_load,
                   seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25),
                   start = decimal_date(as.Date("2021-01-01")))

# Fit TBATS
fit_tbats <- tbats(train_msts)
summary(fit_tbats)

# Forecast 2025
forecast_tbats_2025 <- forecast(fit_tbats, h = nrow(valid_data))

# Accuracy
accuracy(forecast_tbats_2025, as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))

# plot
autoplot(forecast_tbats_2025) +
  autolayer(ts(valid_data$daily_peak_load,
               start = end(train_msts) + c(0, 1),
               frequency = 7),
            series = "Actual",
            color = "red") +
  xlab("Date") +
  ylab("Daily Peak Load") +
  ggtitle("TBATS (Multi-Seasonal): Forecast vs Actual (2025)")

plot_df_tbats <- valid_data %>%
  mutate(forecast = as.numeric(forecast_tbats_2025$mean))

ggplot(plot_df_tbats, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = daily_peak_load, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = forecast, color = "Forecast"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = "TBATS (Multi-Seasonal): Forecast vs Actual (2025)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Daily Peak Load",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
```

## Model 5: Dynamic Harmonic Regression (ARIMA + Fourier Terms)

```{r}
# Step 1: Multi-seasonal ts
train_msts <- msts(train_data$daily_peak_load,
                   seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25),
                   start = decimal_date(as.Date("2021-01-01")))

# Step 2: Find optimal K via AICc (weekly K1=1:3, annual K2=1:5)
best_k    <- c(1, 1)
best_aicc <- Inf

for (k1 in 1:3) {
  for (k2 in 1:5) {
    xreg_k <- cbind(
      fourier(train_msts, K = c(k1, k2)),
      tmax = train_data$tmax_mean
    )
    fit_k <- auto.arima(train_msts,
                        xreg      = xreg_k,
                        seasonal  = FALSE,
                        stepwise  = TRUE,
                        approximation = TRUE)  # faster search
    cat("K =", k1, k2, "| AICc =", fit_k$aicc, "\n")
    if (fit_k$aicc < best_aicc) {
      best_aicc <- fit_k$aicc
      best_k    <- c(k1, k2)
    }
  }
}

cat("\nBest K:", best_k, "\n")

# Step 3: Fit final model with best K + tmax
fourier_train <- cbind(
  fourier(train_msts, K = best_k),
  tmax = train_data$tmax_mean
)

fourier_valid <- cbind(
  fourier(train_msts, K = best_k, h = nrow(valid_data)),
  tmax = valid_data$tmax_mean
)

fit_dhr <- auto.arima(train_msts,
                      xreg      = fourier_train,
                      seasonal  = FALSE,
                      stepwise  = FALSE,
                      approximation = FALSE)

summary(fit_dhr)

# Step 4: Forecast 2025
forecast_dhr_2025 <- forecast(fit_dhr,
                              xreg = fourier_valid,
                              h    = nrow(valid_data))

accuracy(forecast_dhr_2025, as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))

# Step 5: Plot
plot_df_dhr <- valid_data %>%
  mutate(forecast = as.numeric(forecast_dhr_2025$mean))

ggplot(plot_df_dhr, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = daily_peak_load, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = forecast, color = "Forecast"), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("DHR (K=", best_k[1], ",", best_k[2], ") + tmax: Forecast vs Actual (2025)"),
    x = "Date",
    y = "Daily Peak Load",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Model comparison
```{r}
rmse_sarima    <- accuracy(forecast_2025,
                           as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))[2, "RMSE"]

rmse_sarimax   <- accuracy(forecast_sarimax_2025,
                           as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))[2, "RMSE"]

rmse_sarimax_d <- accuracy(forecast_sarimax_m3_2025,
                           as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))[2, "RMSE"]

rmse_tbats     <- accuracy(forecast_tbats_2025,
                           as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))[2, "RMSE"]

rmse_dhr       <- accuracy(forecast_dhr_2025,
                           as.numeric(valid_data$daily_peak_load))[2, "RMSE"]

# Build comparison dataframe
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Model = c("SARIMA (Baseline)",
            "SARIMAX",
            "SARIMAX + Heat Dummy",
            "TBATS",
            "DHR + tmax"),
  RMSE = round(c(rmse_sarima,
                 rmse_sarimax,
                 rmse_sarimax_d,
                 rmse_tbats,
                 rmse_dhr), 2)
) %>%
  arrange(RMSE) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number())

# Print table
print(comparison_df)

# Bar chart
ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = reorder(Model, RMSE), y = RMSE, fill = RMSE)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = format(round(RMSE, 1), big.mark = ",")),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.8, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#2c7bb6", high = "#d7191c") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
                     labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Comparison: RMSE on 2025 Validation Set",
    subtitle = "Lower RMSE = better forecast accuracy",
    x = NULL,
    y = "RMSE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 10),
    axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```

# Summary and Conclusions
